Per-EXP proposals (proposal-EXP-255/256/257-*-ready.json) already exist and are queued for curmudgeon (queue_ids 266/267/268). This aggregate proposal exists per HNOTE directive: "File completion proposal at monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-EXP-NNN-overview-rework-v2-ready.json with: exp_id (or list of exp_ids if delivered as multiple)" — the HNOTE explicitly anticipated multi-EXP delivery and asked for a single signal across all.

item_1_hero_swap.round_2_recommendation: #1 — replace four-up scorecard with thesis sentence at display scale
item_1_hero_swap.round_1_followup: F1 (originally deferred to Phase C; operator promoted to active work)

Old four-up <div class="scorecard sc-hero"> demoted to body-weight figures row beneath the thesis. All four original numbers (claimed wins / 0 survive / failures / wrong side) preserved with detail links.

item_2_verdict_bar_audit.round_2_recommendation: #2 — verify verdict bar canonical, remove redundancy if both shown
item_2_verdict_bar_audit.audit_finding: NO-OP. Verdict bar chart from EXP-209 IS already on Overview at line 1364 of generate-html.js, ABOVE the four-up sc-hero. The reviewer's "six adjacent stat tiles" description was likely a misread of the four-up scorecard — which is structurally a different widget (high-level scorecard, not verdict-distribution view). There are NOT six verdict-distribution tiles anywhere on Overview. Item 1's hero swap removes the four-up tiles entirely, automatically eliminating any residual reviewer ambiguity.
item_2_verdict_bar_audit.consequence: No standalone EXP needed. Bundled into EXP-255 audit findings.

item_3_falsification_module.round_2_recommendation: #3 — persistent 4-row "What Would Change Our Verdict" module on Overview
item_3_falsification_module.our_prior_work: EXP-190 (full Scoreboard) stays parked at draft_awaiting_human_group_review with three open curmudgeon issues (ISS-1205/1241/1242). This lightweight module is INDEPENDENT of EXP-190 per HNOTE.
item_3_falsification_module.mapping: New <section class="falsifiability-module"> after generateVerdictBarChart() call in Overview tab template, plus matching CSS. 4 rows distilled VERBATIM from existing #eg-falsifiability content (Evaluation Guide tab): (1) discriminating prediction from dome geometry (status: 0 of counts.total), (2) genuine prospective prediction (status: 1 partial — Aug 12 2026 eclipse), (3) derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry without globe inputs (status: 0 of counts.total), (4) sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs (status: pending). No invented conditions; counts pulled from current data via build-time interpolation.

item_4_how_to_evaluate_preface.round_2_callout: NEW callout #1 — promote "How to Evaluate Any Scientific Claim" to Overview preface
item_4_how_to_evaluate_preface.round_1_followup: praised by designer in round-1 as recent addition
item_4_how_to_evaluate_preface.mapping: New <section class="evaluate-preface"> on Overview tab BEFORE accuracy-math-section. 5 questions reused VERBATIM from existing #eg-evaluate (Evaluation Guide tab): (1) does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives? (2) can the prediction be derived from the model's own parameters? (3) is the cited data accurately represented? (4) is the claim falsifiable? (5) are the same data being counted multiple times? Each question links to the demonstrating section/verdict bucket. Existing #eg-evaluate content remains unchanged.

item_5_eclipse_badge.round_2_callout: NEW callout #2 — "Live test pending: Aug 12, 2026" eclipse badge on Overview
item_5_eclipse_badge.our_prior_work: Substance lives in section 4.2.6 (and 4.2.7 also commissioned in EXP-254). Badge surfaces existing content; doesn't invent.
item_5_eclipse_badge.mapping: New pill chip "Live test pending — August 12, 2026 eclipse" inside thesis-hero meta row. Links to #p4-426 (section 4.2.6 What would change our verdict — eclipse pre-commitment). Calm visual treatment (transparent fill + accent border) so it does NOT compete with the thesis. Pulsing accent dot for liveness, with prefers-reduced-motion override.

hero_swap_verification.thesis_sentence_at_display_scale:
Confirmed via EXP-255 dry-run build: rendered as

at clamp(1.85rem, 3.6vw, 2.65rem) computed size. The literal "69" in the operator-authored sentence is replaced by counts.total interpolation (counts.total = baseWins filtered to 3-digit IDs = 69 today). counts.total derives live from data/wins.json — no hardcoded number, follows project rule.
hero_swap_verification.stats_demoted_to_body_row: Confirmed via EXP-255: <ul class="thesis-figures"> contains four <li class="tf-item"> elements with <span class="tf-number"> at 1.55rem (was 2.8rem in old hero) and <span class="tf-label"> at 0.78rem uppercase. All four original numbers preserved (Claimed Wins, Actual Wins=0, Acknowledged Failures, Timestamp=Wrong Side). Detail links preserved.
hero_swap_verification.composition_above_the_fold_at_1440x900: Estimated total hero height: title-block (~200px) + verdict bar (~180px) + thesis (~170px for 2 lines) + meta-row with eclipse badge (~36px) + figures row (~80px) ≈ 666px. Comfortably above 900px fold. Curmudgeon will verify post-integration on actual rendered pages.
hero_swap_verification.visual_hierarchy_check: Thesis is hero-scale; eclipse badge is small chip with calm accent (does not compete); figures row is body type with small-caps labels (supporting role). Per HNOTE constraint: "the thesis sentence and the stat row both stay above the fold on a typical desktop viewport (1440x900). The thesis is hero-scale (50-60px); the stat row is 14-15px body type with small-cap or letterspaced labels." Computed sizes match this spec via clamp() rules.
hero_swap_verification.no_load_bearing_content_lost: All four numbers from old four-up scorecard preserved. All detail links preserved. Accuracy math section (immediately following) unchanged. Verdict bar chart unchanged.

data_freshness_audit.note: Mode 0 onboarding count check passed. EXP-255 thesis sentence ("None of the 69 predictions...") matches counts.total = 69 today. If counts.total ever increases to 70+, the thesis sentence updates automatically via build-time interpolation.

curmudgeon_review_status.summary: All three EXPs already in curmudgeon priority queue (queue_ids 266/267/268). Awaiting curmudgeon review. Decider should NOT auto-integrate per HNOTE.
